idea-plugin.name: Jeffrey Microscope
idea-plugin.description:
Jump from a JFR flame graph straight to your code.

Jeffrey Microscope is the companion IDE plugin for Jeffrey, a tool for analyzing Java Flight Recorder (JFR) recordings and heap dumps. While you explore a flame graph, a heap-dump view, or any other Microscope view, this plugin lets you click a stack frame or a class and land on the exact line of source in your already-open IntelliJ window.

The plugin is headless — it adds no toolbars, tool windows, or actions of its own. It runs quietly in the background and enriches Jeffrey Microscope, letting Microscope discover your open IntelliJ projects and either navigate straight to the matching code or download the sources back into the application directly.

What it does

  • Frame-to-source navigation — resolves a fully-qualified class and method to a file, line, and column, opens it, and brings the IDE window to the front.
  • Inline source — serves source text back to Microscope so it can show code next to the profile, preferring attached sources over decompiled .class files.
  • Zero configuration — Microscope discovers the IDE automatically over IntelliJ's built-in server; there is nothing to pair or paste.

Private by design

  • Listens on localhost only — never reachable from the network.
  • Honors IntelliJ's trusted projects: untrusted projects are never navigated.
  • Toggle it off anytime under Settings → Tools → Jeffrey Microscope Plugin; a disabled IDE is completely invisible to Microscope.

Requires a running Jeffrey Microscope instance. Learn more at jeffrey-analyst.cafe.
